subject: Computer Networking
title: The Dot-Com Boom and Bust
description: A fast breakdown of The Dot-Com Boom and Bust for builders.

hook.text: In the late 1990s, adding '.com' to your company name doubled your stock price. The hype was surreal.

buildup.text: Investors poured billions into web startups. Business models didn't matter — 'get big fast' was the only strategy.

discovery.text: Companies like Amazon, eBay, and Google launched during this era. So did Pets.com, Webvan, and Kozmo.

twist.text: The bubble burst in 2000. The NASDAQ lost 78% of its value. Hundreds of dot-coms vanished overnight.

climax.text: The survivors — Amazon, Google, eBay — went on to dominate the global economy for decades.

punchline.text: The bubble was wrong about timing, not about the internet's potential.

quiz.question: What happened to the NASDAQ during the dot-com bust?
options: It lost about 78% of its value
